spec_plan.title: Reference Context for Stdlib Test Coverage — Lower Coverage Tier
spec_plan.summary: The mamba-stdlib-coverage-lower group targets 10 lowest-coverage stdlib modules (queue_mod, statistics_mod, shlex_mod, calendar_mod, locale_mod, lzma_mod, zlib_mod, secrets_mod, bisect_mod, abc_mod) ranging from 4% to 14% coverage. Per pre-clarifications, these map to 6-7 existing stdlib spec groups. This reference context documents the specification-driven foundation for each module, test infrastructure, and coverage targets (100% line + branch).

in_scope: Inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests for per-function branch paths
Self-contained tests: no network, no external files
Real C bindings with fixed in-memory byte arrays (lzma/zlib)
Concurrent tests using std::thread or tokio::task (queue_mod)
Target: 100% line + 100% branch coverage per cargo-llvm-cov --branch
Mapping to existing stdlib spec groups (datetime, typing-and-inspect, concurrency, numeric, archive-and-compression, text-processing, hashlib)
out_of_scope: Platform-specific guards and dead code removal

sections.name: overview
sections.reasoning: Provides crate structure and spec organization for locating stdlib modules under runtime/stdlib/. Helps contextualize how specs map to source files.

sections.name: target-module-datetime
sections.reasoning: calendar_mod is date/time functionality. Spec defines datetime, date, timedelta classes with arithmetic operations and strftime formatting. Critical for understanding calendar module requirements and API surface.

sections.name: target-module-typing
sections.reasoning: abc_mod provides abstract base classes and introspection. Spec covers typing type aliases and inspect methods (isfunction, isclass, ismethod, getmembers, signature). Essential for ABC implementation and testing reflection capabilities.

sections.name: target-module-concurrency
sections.reasoning: queue_mod implements thread-safe queue operations. Spec defines threading.Thread, Lock, Event, subprocess.run/Popen primitives. Provides foundation for concurrent queue testing with synchronization patterns.

sections.name: target-module-numeric
sections.reasoning: statistics_mod and bisect_mod are numeric operations. Spec covers Decimal, Fraction, array types. statistics and bisect are algorithms operating on numeric sequences. Defines numeric type constraints and edge cases.

sections.name: target-module-archive
sections.reasoning: lzma_mod and zlib_mod are compression utilities. Spec mentions lzma/zlib as deferred but covers gzip/zipfile/tarfile compression ecosystem. Provides patterns for compress/decompress round-trip testing with in-memory buffers.

sections.name: target-module-text
sections.reasoning: shlex_mod provides shell-like string lexing. Spec covers textwrap and string constants. Related to text parsing and string manipulation patterns. shlex conceptually fits text-processing ecosystem.

sections.name: target-module-secrets
sections.reasoning: secrets_mod generates cryptographically secure random tokens. hashlib spec covers hash algorithms and incremental updates (used in token generation). random spec defines PRNG interface. Secrets contrasts with general randomness and emphasizes security.

sections.name: testing-infrastructure
sections.reasoning: Defines Mamba's test infrastructure with fixture discovery, directive dispatch (parse/typecheck/jit), and error reporting. Essential for understanding how inline #[cfg(test)] modules and integration tests fit into cargo-llvm-cov coverage measurement.

sections.name: stdlib-context
sections.reasoning: Discusses stdlib modernization via native Rust crates. Provides background on how stdlib modules are rewritten for robustness. Contextualizes why high-quality test coverage is important for production-grade implementations.
